seoTitle: Chemi-Con Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitors Product Catalog | BeiLuo Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Chemi-Con aluminum electrolytic capacitors, radial lead, snap-in, screw terminal, and automotive capacitors. Technical specifications, selection guide, and alternative parts.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Chemi-Con capacitor for my application?
faqs.answer: Consider voltage rating, capacitance value, ripple current requirements, temperature range, and lifetime requirements. Chemi-Con offers comprehensive selection guides for each product series. For industrial applications, KMQ series provides excellent reliability. For automotive, select AEC-Q200 qualified series. For high-power applications, consider snap-in or screw terminal types.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application specifications for personalized Chemi-Con capacitor selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Chemi-Con's KMQ and KZE series?
faqs.answer: KMQ series is Chemi-Con's premium line with highest reliability and longest lifetime, ideal for demanding industrial and automotive applications. KZE series offers excellent performance at competitive pricing for general-purpose applications. Both series feature Japan manufacturing quality but target different application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use KMQ series for mission-critical applications. Use KZE series for cost-sensitive general-purpose applications.

faqs.question: What are Chemi-Con's automotive-grade capacitors?
faqs.answer: Chemi-Con's automotive-grade capacitors are AEC-Q200 qualified and designed specifically for vehicle applications. These capacitors undergo rigorous testing including temperature cycling, vibration, and mechanical shock. They feature extended temperature ratings up to 150°C and enhanced reliability for automotive electronics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications requiring AEC-Q200 qualification, select Chemi-Con's automotive series capacitors.

faqs.question: Are Chemi-Con capacitors suitable for renewable energy applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chemi-Con capacitors are widely used in solar inverters, wind power systems, and energy storage applications. The high reliability and long lifetime of Chemi-Con capacitors make them ideal for renewable energy systems where maintenance access may be difficult. KMQ series is particularly popular for these applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for recommendations on Chemi-Con capacitors for renewable energy applications.

faqs.question: What is the quality difference between Japan-made and overseas-made Chemi-Con capacitors?
faqs.answer: Chemi-Con maintains consistent quality standards across all manufacturing locations. However, premium series such as KMQ are manufactured in Japan using the most advanced production equipment and processes. For mission-critical applications, specify Japan-made capacitors for maximum reliability assurance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum reliability, specify Japan-made Chemi-Con capacitors, particularly the KMQ series.

categories.name: Radial Lead Capacitors
categories.description: High-reliability radial aluminum electrolytic capacitors for general-purpose applications
categories.longDescription: Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors represent the industry standard for quality and reliability. The KMQ series is widely regarded as the benchmark for high-reliability aluminum electrolytic capacitors. These capacitors feature Chemi-Con's advanced electrode technology and proprietary electrolyte formulation, ensuring exceptional performance and long operational life. Available in a wide range of capacitance and voltage ratings, Chemi-Con radial capacitors are the preferred choice for industrial, automotive, and commercial applications. Contact BeiLuo Electronics, your authorized Chemi-Con distributor, for radial capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, size constraints, and lifetime requirements. KMQ series for highest reliability, KZE for general purpose, KY for high temperature.

selectionGuideLink.text: View Radial Lead Capacitors Selection Guide

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors?
faqs.answer: Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors are widely used in power supplies, industrial controls, consumer electronics, and automotive applications. They provide reliable energy storage, filtering, and decoupling functions. The specific application depends on the capacitance value, voltage rating, and package type selected. Chemi-Con's reputation for quality makes these capacitors ideal for mission-critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product catalog to find the right Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors for your application, or contact our FAE team for selection assistance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors for my design?
faqs.answer: When selecting Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors, consider these key parameters: voltage rating (with 20-50% derating), capacitance value, ripple current requirements, temperature range, lifetime requirements, and physical size constraints. Also evaluate ESR characteristics for high-frequency applications and consider the mounting style for your PCB design. Chemi-Con provides comprehensive datasheets to aid selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection guide or contact our FAE team with your specifications for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors?
faqs.answer: The lifetime of Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors varies by series and operating conditions. Standard series typically offer 2,000 to 5,000 hours at rated temperature, while high-temperature series can provide 10,000 hours or more. Actual lifetime depends heavily on operating temperature and voltage derating - following the Arrhenius relationship where every 10°C reduction doubles the lifetime. Chemi-Con capacitors are known for exceeding rated lifetimes in actual applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For specific lifetime calculations based on your operating conditions, contact our FAE team.

faqs.question: Are Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors RoHS compliant and environmentally friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors are RoHS compliant and free from lead, mercury, cadmium, and other restricted substances. They meet international environmental standards and are suitable for worldwide distribution. Chemi-Con also complies with REACH regulations and maintains ISO 14001 environmental management certification.
faqs.decisionGuide: For specific environmental compliance documentation, contact our sales team with your part numbers.

faqs.question: What makes Chemi-Con Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors superior to competitors?
faqs.answer: Chemi-Con Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors benefit from over 90 years of Japanese manufacturing expertise, advanced electrode foil technology, proprietary electrolyte formulations, and rigorous quality control. These factors result in capacitors with lower ESR, longer lifetime, and higher reliability compared to competitors. Chemi-Con's KMQ series is widely regarded as the industry benchmark.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request samples to evaluate Chemi-Con quality in your application.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard Chemi-Con radial lead capacitors typically have 6-8 weeks lead time from Japan. Popular series may be available from local stock at BeiLuo Electronics for immediate delivery. For large volume orders or custom specifications, lead time may be 10-12 weeks. Contact our sales team for current stock availability and scheduled delivery options.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent requirements, check our local stock or discuss scheduled orders with our sales team.

products.name: Radial Capacitor 1000uF 25V
products.shortDescription: KMQ-1000uF-25V aluminum electrolytic capacitor with 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating for industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The KMQ-1000uF-25V is a high-quality aluminum electrolytic capacitor from Chemi-Con, Japan's premier capacitor manufacturer. With 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating, it provides reliable performance for demanding applications.

This capacitor features Chemi-Con's advanced electrode technology and proprietary electrolyte formulation, ensuring long operational life and stable performance. The robust construction withstands harsh operating conditions.
Rated for 105°C operation with 1.5A ripple current capability, this capacitor is suitable for industrial, automotive, and commercial applications requiring high reliability.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my extensive experience with Chemi-Con capacitors, the KMQ-1000uF-25V consistently delivers exceptional performance. The 1000uF/25V rating is well-suited for a wide range of applications. Chemi-Con's Japan manufacturing quality ensures reliable operation even in demanding conditions. I particularly appreciate the low ESR of 0.02 Ohm, which minimizes self-heating and improves overall efficiency.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent choice for high-reliability radial lead applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where lower capacitance is acceptable

alternativeParts.reason: Higher voltage rating for increased safety margin
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring higher voltage margin

applicationScenarios.description: Output filtering for industrial power supply using KMQ-1000uF-25V
Filtering for automotive electronic control unit

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the KMQ-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 1.5A at 105°C and 120Hz. This rating ensures reliable operation in power supply applications with moderate switching frequencies. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 1.8A at 85°C. For high-frequency applications above 100kHz, the effective ripple current capability may be reduced due to increased ESR. Always verify the actual operating temperature and frequency conditions in your application to ensure the capacitor operates within its safe operating area.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 1.5A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or upgrading to a higher capacitance model. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and ripple current calculations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the KMQ-1000uF-25V at typical operating conditions?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 105°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, we can calculate the expected lifetime at different operating temperatures. At 85°C, the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 65°C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. At 25°C ambient, you can achieve up to 128,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction in operating temperature approximately doubles the capacitor lifetime. These calculations assume voltage derating to 80% of rated voltage and ripple current within specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum capacitor lifetime, operate at the lowest practical temperature with 80% voltage derating. Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations specific to your application conditions.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for reliable operation of the KMQ-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: Industry best practice recommends operating aluminum electrolytic capacitors at no more than 80% of their rated voltage. For this 25V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 20V. This 20% derating significantly improves reliability and extends operational lifetime. For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 13V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the KMQ-1000uF-25V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40°C to +105°C. At the maximum rated temperature of 105°C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10°C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the KMQ-1000uF-25V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.02 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple KMQ-1000uF-25V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting KMQ-1000uF-25V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 2000uF capacitance and approximately 0.010 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 3.0A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.

The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the LXS-4700uF-100V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40°C to +105°C. At the maximum rated temperature of 105°C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10°C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the LXS-4700uF-100V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.00 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple LXS-4700uF-100V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting LXS-4700uF-100V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 9400uF capacitance and approximately 0.002 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 9.6A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.

categories.name: Screw Terminal Capacitors
categories.description: Large can screw terminal capacitors for high-power industrial applications
categories.longDescription: Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors are designed for the most demanding high-power applications including industrial drives, renewable energy systems, and large UPS installations. These large can capacitors feature screw terminals for secure high-current connections and robust construction for long-term reliability. Available in very high capacitance values up to 680,000uF and voltage ratings up to 500VDC. Contact BeiLuo Electronics, your authorized Chemi-Con distributor, for screw terminal capacitor selection.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, ripple current, and mounting requirements. ALS series for standard, ALE for extended life.

selectionGuideLink.text: View Screw Terminal Capacitors Selection Guide

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors?
faqs.answer: Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors are widely used in power supplies, industrial controls, consumer electronics, and automotive applications. They provide reliable energy storage, filtering, and decoupling functions. The specific application depends on the capacitance value, voltage rating, and package type selected. Chemi-Con's reputation for quality makes these capacitors ideal for mission-critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product catalog to find the right Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors for your application, or contact our FAE team for selection assistance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors for my design?
faqs.answer: When selecting Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors, consider these key parameters: voltage rating (with 20-50% derating), capacitance value, ripple current requirements, temperature range, lifetime requirements, and physical size constraints. Also evaluate ESR characteristics for high-frequency applications and consider the mounting style for your PCB design. Chemi-Con provides comprehensive datasheets to aid selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection guide or contact our FAE team with your specifications for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors?
faqs.answer: The lifetime of Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors varies by series and operating conditions. Standard series typically offer 2,000 to 5,000 hours at rated temperature, while high-temperature series can provide 10,000 hours or more. Actual lifetime depends heavily on operating temperature and voltage derating - following the Arrhenius relationship where every 10°C reduction doubles the lifetime. Chemi-Con capacitors are known for exceeding rated lifetimes in actual applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For specific lifetime calculations based on your operating conditions, contact our FAE team.

faqs.question: Are Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors RoHS compliant and environmentally friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors are RoHS compliant and free from lead, mercury, cadmium, and other restricted substances. They meet international environmental standards and are suitable for worldwide distribution. Chemi-Con also complies with REACH regulations and maintains ISO 14001 environmental management certification.
faqs.decisionGuide: For specific environmental compliance documentation, contact our sales team with your part numbers.

faqs.question: What makes Chemi-Con Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors superior to competitors?
faqs.answer: Chemi-Con Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors benefit from over 90 years of Japanese manufacturing expertise, advanced electrode foil technology, proprietary electrolyte formulations, and rigorous quality control. These factors result in capacitors with lower ESR, longer lifetime, and higher reliability compared to competitors. Chemi-Con's KMQ series is widely regarded as the industry benchmark.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request samples to evaluate Chemi-Con quality in your application.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard Chemi-Con screw terminal capacitors typically have 6-8 weeks lead time from Japan. Popular series may be available from local stock at BeiLuo Electronics for immediate delivery. For large volume orders or custom specifications, lead time may be 10-12 weeks. Contact our sales team for current stock availability and scheduled delivery options.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent requirements, check our local stock or discuss scheduled orders with our sales team.

This capacitor features Chemi-Con's advanced electrode technology and proprietary electrolyte formulation, ensuring long operational life and stable performance. The robust construction withstands harsh operating conditions.

faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics

The 47000uF/100V rating is well-suited for a wide range of applications. Chemi-Con's Japan manufacturing quality ensures reliable operation even in demanding conditions. applicationScenarios.description: Output filtering for industrial power supply using ALS-47000uF-100V
Filtering for automotive electronic control unit

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the ALS-47000uF-100V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 15.0A at 85°C and 120Hz. This rating ensures reliable operation in power supply applications with moderate switching frequencies. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 18.0A at 65°C. For high-frequency applications above 100kHz, the effective ripple current capability may be reduced due to increased ESR. Always verify the actual operating temperature and frequency conditions in your application to ensure the capacitor operates within its safe operating area.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 15.0A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or upgrading to a higher capacitance model. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and ripple current calculations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the ALS-47000uF-100V at typical operating conditions?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, we can calculate the expected lifetime at different operating temperatures. At 65°C, the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 45°C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. At 25°C ambient, you can achieve up to 128,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction in operating temperature approximately doubles the capacitor lifetime. These calculations assume voltage derating to 80% of rated voltage and ripple current within specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum capacitor lifetime, operate at the lowest practical temperature with 80% voltage derating. Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations specific to your application conditions.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for reliable operation of the ALS-47000uF-100V?
faqs.answer: Industry best practice recommends operating aluminum electrolytic capacitors at no more than 80% of their rated voltage. For this 100V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 80V. This 20% derating significantly improves reliability and extends operational lifetime. For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 50V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10°C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the ALS-47000uF-100V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.00 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple ALS-47000uF-100V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting ALS-47000uF-100V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 94000uF capacitance and approximately 0.001 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 30.0A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.

For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 32V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the AKM-470uF-63V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40°C to +125°C. At the maximum rated temperature of 125°C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10°C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the AKM-470uF-63V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.02 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple AKM-470uF-63V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting AKM-470uF-63V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 940uF capacitance and approximately 0.009 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 2.6A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.
